ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-28-2019 Continued To Feb-25-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Benjamin Holl for David Parker (Administrator).

The Court noted that the overpayment for the mortgage was not addressed in the amended petition. Mr. Holl stated that there is some confusion and that he believes the issue of the mortgage was dealt with in the conservatorship. The Court informed Mr. Holl that it disagrees with him. Mr. Holl requested more time for the refinance to be completed, stated that they can then deal with the issue of the mortgage, that it is supposed to be funded in two weeks and that he is not sure why it was held up. The First and Final Report on Waiver of Account is continued to February 25, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Benjamin Holl for David Parker (Petitioner)

The Court stated that the estate needs to be closed in a timely fashion. Mr. Holl requested a 3-month continuance. The petition for approval of status of administration is continued to August 7, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204, which is 3 months from the date they filed the report.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jun-03-2015 Continued To Jul-08-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-06-2015 Continued To Jun-03-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Parker in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that it looks as if there will be issues going forward, urged Mr. Parker to obtain counsel, that the accountings in the conservatorship have given the court concern and that the estate looks as if there could be complications. Mr. Parker stated that he feels the estated will be easier than the conservatorship, that he has consulted with an attorney and will do so during these proceedings, that he does not think he needs counsel, that it will be costly, that he has 14 years experience with the conservatorship, he has a Title V education code, that the heirs are in agreement with everything. Mr. Parker further stated that his next steps would be to open an estate account, send out creditor's notices, notify the Health and Welfare Department, notify FTB (although not expecting taxes due), that he does not expect any creditor's claims, that they will want to waive accountings and will then file for final distribution. The Court expressed concern in regards to Mr. Parker representing himself, stressed that notice will be critical, that it is not sure the other beneficiaries will waive accountings and that if Mr. Parker is not represented by counsel by June 3, 2015 he will need a broader plan. Mr. Parker stated that this is not a large estate, that the only asset is the house, that they are all living in the house and want to keep it and that the statutory fees will be too high. The petition for letters of administration was continued to June 3, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Lopes. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Apr-21-2015 Continued To May-06-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
